task.title: Working with large filters
task.shortdesc: To improve performance for large filters, you can limit the number of data sets that are displayed in the Remote Systems view.

keywords.indexterm: Remote Systems viewworking with large filtersfilterslarge

taskbody.prereq: To set preferences for working with large filters, see .
taskbody.context: When the default settings for the MVS Files subsystem are in effect and a filter is expanded, the Remote Systems view displays a maximum of 50 data sets. You can take the following actions to browse through the data sets.The examples use a smaller filter for purposes of illustration.
When you first expand a large filter, the data sets in excess of 50 are shown by using placeholders:Large filter placeholder

step.cmd: To show more data sets, double-click the placeholder.
step.stepresult: The next 50 data sets are displayed and a placeholder is shown for the previous 50 data sets: Large filter with two placeholders

step.cmd: To continue to browse up and down through the data sets, double-click either the top or the bottom placeholder.
step.info:
You can also browse through data sets by doing the following actions:
Select a filter name and then select Navigate Top, Navigate Bottom, Navigate Previous, or Navigate Next from the menu.
Select a filter name, data set name, or placeholder and then press the keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+Home (top), Ctrl+End (bottom), Ctrl+PgUp (previous), or Ctrl+PgDn (next).

taskbody.prereq: To use the Locate command, you must set the Sort Order for Data Sets option on the MVS Files Preference page to By name. Otherwise, the sort order is changed to By name temporarily until the filter is refreshed.

steps.stepsection: To locate a specific data set, do these steps.

step.cmd: Select the filter name, and then select Locate from the menu or press keyboard shortcut Ctrl+L.
In the Locate Data Set window, specify the data set name that you want to locate and click OK.
step.info: You can specify a partial data set name, such as HLQ. If the name you search for does not exist, the data set name just before the search string is located. Previous searches are preserved and added to a list in the Locate Data Set window. You can select a previous search string from this list.
step.stepresult: The data set name is displayed and highlighted: Located data set

task.title: Changing the number of data sets to display

steps-unordered.stepsection: To change the numbers of data sets to display for a filter, do one of the following actions:

step.cmd: Select a placeholder and then select Show More or Show Less from the menu. A window opens prompting you to specify the number of data sets to display.
Select a filter name and then select Properties from the menu. On the Properties page, select Expansion and specify the number of data sets to display in the Expansion page size field.
